title: Sources: Ravens to acquire Crosby in blockbuster

content: Jimmy Wales says AI can contribute by making translations better but warns that “even a good machine translation” can fall short of expectations if “cultural questions” are not addressed

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹60 amid West Asia conflict
content: Non-subsidised LPG, the one that common household users other than the Ujjwala beneficiaries use in their kitchens, will now cost ₹913 per 14.2 kg cylinder in Delhi, according to the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) website; the price increase is effective from March 7

title: 204 of 238 Indian cities did not meet air quality standards: CREA
content: Ghaziabad was the most polluted city, recording an average PM2.5 concentration of 172 g/m³, followed by Noida (166 g/m³), and Delhi (163 g/m³), as per CREA, a Finland-based independent research group.

title: In a Bengal college, an Iftar party to be held by women for women
content: The event, called ‘2026 Dosti ki Iftar’ and being organised by the Deeniyat Muallima College (located in Santragachi, across the river from Kolkata) and the city-based initiative Know Your Neighbour, intends to “provide a space for women to exchanges their views, and break myths and stereotypes”.

title: Fadnavis presents ₹7.69 lakh crore Maharashtra Budget, loan waiver for farmers
content: The Budget proposes a ninefold growth of the agricultural economy, aiming to raise the sector’s GDP from $55 billion to $500 billion by 2047. The State’s revenue receipts are estimated at around ₹6.56 lakh crore, resulting in a revenue deficit of about ₹40,500 crore.

title: Andhra Pradesh firecracker unit blast: Gone in a flash fire
content: An explosion at a firecracker manufacturing unit in Vetlapalem village of Kakinada district, Andhra Pradesh, claimed 28 workers’ lives, including eight women. Preliminary investigations reveal that the unit’s owners had allegedly stored explosive materials far beyond the permitted limit and engaged 31 workers, nearly four times the number allowed under licence, in a bid to fulfil festival and wedding orders, reports T. Appala Naidu

title: Why does India require U.S. nod for securing its energy needs, ask Opposition leaders
content: The Opposition leaders question the government’s silence over U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s latest remark objecting to the tone of his announcement ‘allowing’ India a temporary waiver to purchase Russian oil

title: U.S. waiver to procure Russian oil: Kerala flays Centre
content: It is outrageous that a country like India should appear to require approval from the U.S. for decisions such as oil imports, says Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan in a post on X

title: Youth, student groups criticise Karnataka Budget over jobs, education funding
content: Student and youth organisations have criticised the State Budget for 2026-27, alleging that it fails to address the pressing issues of unemployment among youth and the declining state of public education

title: YSRCP shirking responsibility of raising public issues in Assembly, says A.P. CM
content: The ₹3.32 lakh-crore budget has been approved in the Assembly, but a concerted effort is needed to spend every single rupee judiciously, says Naidu

content: Dipali Das, who entered India in February 1988, was declared a non-citizen by the Foreigners’ Tribunal in Silchar

content: All India Civil Services Coaching Centre, the institute of the State government, has registered an increase in results owing to Naan Mudhalvan Scheme.

title: Pinarayi slams Centre’s ‘submission’ before US over strikes on Iran
content: The country being tied to imperial powers is a matter of concern, and strong public opinion should arise against it, says Chief Minister

title: Watch: Iranian warship strike near Sri Lanka raises questions for India
content: An Iranian warship was struck by a U.S. submarine near Sri Lanka, days after participating in India’s MILAN 2026 naval exercise. The incident raises major questions about India’s role in the Indian Ocean and the strategic balance between Washington and Tehran.

title: West Asia conflict: Airlines bring back 15,000 stranded passengers

title: Budget reflects State’s continued focus on diverse growth: trade bodies
content: FKCCI welcomes several important initiatives aimed at strengthening the industrial ecosystem and creating new opportunities for investment, innovation, and employment

title: India granted emergency docking to Iranian naval ship in Kochi, days before attack on IRIS Dena
content: IRIS Lavan has been docked at Kochi since March 4 as the vessel had developed technical issues. “Its crew of 183 are currently accommodated at naval facilities in Kochi,” the source said

title: India must ask U.S. why it is targeting Iranian ships in Indian Ocean: Iran Minister Khatibzadeh
content: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh says his country is a “responsible power” and has not shut the Strait of Hormuz, he says the attack on Iran is “based on a lie” and pitches for diplomacy as the “only option”

title: Proposal to rename Victoria Hospital in Bengaluru takes doctors, patients by surprise
content: Doctors at the hospital say they had no prior information about the proposal until it was announced in the Budget

title: Karnataka Budget 2026–27: Capex slows as committed expenses balloon and revenue takes a hit
content: Karnataka pins hopes on raising its own tax revenue and increasing borrowing to push capital expenditure

title: 'Wanted to come into our port': EAM Jaishankar on India's rescue of Iranian ships
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar stated that India granted permission for an Iranian warship facing difficulties to dock in Kochi, guided by humanitarian principles. He noted that another Iranian vessel, IRIS Dena, was caught on the "wrong side of events" near Sri Lanka and was subsequently sunk by the US Navy.

title: Iran Prez apolgises for strikes on Gulf states; rejects Trump's 'unconditional surrender' call
content: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has apologized for recent strikes on neighboring nations. He stated Iran will not attack any country unless attacked first. This comes as an interim leadership council now governs the Islamic Republic. Pezeshkian also rejected calls for Iran's unconditional surrender. The foreign minister suggested the Revolutionary Guard Corps is operating independently.

title: Can Surya do what Dhoni, Kohli couldn't? This T20 WC stat vs NZ may worry India
content: India faces New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final, aiming to defend their title and become three-time champions. Despite a previous loss to South Africa, India reached the final after a strong semi-final win. New Zealand, however, holds a perfect 3-0 record against India in T20 World Cups, seeking their maiden title.

title: ‘He came to save us’: Family says Delhi youth was lured on Holi, beaten to death by mob
content: A neighbourhood spat over Holi colours turned deadly in Delhi, resulting in the alleged beating death of 26-year-old Tarun Butolia. Tarun was reportedly lured back home by a call claiming his parents were under attack, only to be ambushed by a mob. The violence has a communal undertone, with neighbours from different communities involved.

title: After denying delay in data centre project with OpenAI for months, Oracle goes ahead and scraps it
content: Oracle and OpenAI have scrapped plans to expand their flagship Stargate data centre in Abilene, Texas, after months of failed negotiations over financing and shifting demand. The collapse comes despite Oracle repeatedly insisting its OpenAI relationship was on track. The company now sits on over $100 billion in debt, faces potential layoffs of up to 30,000 employees, and has seen its stock halve since September 2025.

title: Explained: How Iran war is driving sharp rise in oil and gas prices
content: Global oil prices have surged dramatically this week. The ongoing conflict involving Iran has intensified, raising fears of prolonged energy supply disruptions from the Middle East. Key energy routes and facilities are disrupted. This has pushed oil and gas prices higher across global markets. Consumers and businesses are facing rising fuel costs.

title: Attack Chakravarthy and his confidence drops, Ashwin warns ahead of final
content: Ravichandran Ashwin observed Varun Chakravarthy's bowling struggles, noting a dip in confidence when attacked early.  Despite a wicket, Chakravarthy conceded 64 runs in the T20 World Cup semi-final, a costly spell. Ashwin advised tactical adjustments and warned against bowling round the wicket to left-handers, emphasizing the need for improved length and line variations.

title: Microsoft becomes first company to say it is not 'abandoning' Anthropic
content: Microsoft will continue using Anthropic's AI models, including Claude, for its customers despite the US Department of War labeling the startup a supply-chain risk.  The tech giant's legal team reviewed the designation, concluding most clients can still access the technology. This decision comes after the Pentagon's move to phase out Anthropic's systems, sparking debate over AI in defense.

title: In rage after neighbour hurt her son, Trichy woman hurls puppies against wall; 2 die
content: A Trichy woman, enraged after her neighbor allegedly injured her son, killed two newborn stray puppies she mistakenly believed were being cared for by the neighbor. CCTV footage of the brutal act sparked public outcry. Both women have been arrested, with charges including cruelty to animals and causing injury.

title: Pakistan's Asim Munir meets Saudi defence minister: Is 'Islamic Nato' in play?
content: Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir met Saudi Defence Minister Khalid bin Salman amidst escalating Middle East tensions following Iranian attacks. The meeting discussed halting these strikes under their Joint Strategic Defense Agreement, highlighting concerns for regional security. This follows reports of Turkey seeking a defense alliance with Pakistan and Saudi Arabia, potentially forming an 'Islamic Nato'.

title: US clears $151.8m sale of 12,000 bomb casings to Israel as conflict with Iran escalates
content: The US has greenlit an emergency $151.8 million arms sale of bomb casings to Israel amidst escalating Middle East conflict. This move, bypassing Congress, aims to bolster Israel's defense and deter regional threats. The sale includes crucial support services, with some equipment drawn from US stockpiles, as fighting intensifies between Israel and Iran.

title: Workers urge Amazon, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI to set limits on US military ties
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that could compromise AI safety guardrails.  The open letter, spearheaded by 'No Tech For Apartheid,' urges leaders to refuse demands that would allow mass surveillance and AI-powered weaponry without human oversight.

title: 'Cracking inflation whip on public': Opposition slams Centre over LPG cylinder price hike
content: Opposition parties have sharply criticised the BJP-led government following a Rs 60 increase in domestic LPG cylinder prices and Rs 115 for commercial cylinders. Leaders from Congress and TMC accused Prime Minister Modi of burdening households with rising costs, while the government assured sufficient fuel supplies amidst West Asia tensions.

title: Open kitchens to private retreats: How home architectural styles are witnessing a shift in 2026
content: Home design is shifting from purely aesthetic appeal to prioritizing functionality and lifestyle integration. Modern homeowners now seek comfort, flexibility, and privacy, with spaces adapting to hybrid work and wellness needs. This evolution redefines luxury, emphasizing emotional balance and personal well-being within the home.

title: Watch: US strikes 3,000 targets in Iran in 7 days of Operation Epic Fury
content: US forces have reportedly struck over 3,000 targets in Operation Epic Fury's first week amidst escalating conflict with Iran. President Trump demanded Iran's unconditional surrender, while Israel launched airstrikes in Lebanon and Iran retaliated with drone and missile attacks.  Regional tensions are soaring, with significant casualties and displacement reported.

title: IND vs NZ final: What happens if rain washes out Ahmedabad clash?
content: India national cricket team will face New Zealand national cricket team in the 2026 T20 World Cup final at Narendra Modi Stadium, aiming to defend their title after beating England in the semifinal. If rain disrupts the match, officials will try to finish it the same day or use a reserve day. If no result is possible, both teams share the title. A tie will be decided through repeated Super Overs until a winner emerges.

title: Inside the paradox of Mexico's never-ending war on drugs
content: The decades-long war on drugs, initiated by Nixon, has devolved into a geopolitical quagmire. The recent death of cartel leader El Mencho, hailed as a victory, quickly led to widespread chaos, demonstrating how successes in combating drug trafficking often breed deeper crises and more violence across Latin America.

title: Shoaib Akhtar calls England player 'goron ka Hardik Pandya'
content: Shoaib Akhtar praised Will Jacks after his all-round effort against India national cricket team in the T20 World Cup semifinal, calling him “Goron ka Hardik Pandya.” Jacks took two wickets, including Sanju Samson, and scored 35 runs. Despite his performance, England lost by seven runs as India advanced to the final.

title: Gukesh apologises to fans; Indian stars slip in rankings: what’s going wrong?
content: Gukesh Dommaraju’s emotional apology during the Prague Masters highlighted a broader dip in form among India’s top players, including Arjun Erigaisi and Rameshbabu Praggnanandhaa. Experts cite heavy schedules, distractions from fame, repetitive elite events and overreliance on engine preparation as factors. It is also likely that the slump is temporary.

title: 'Dhurandhar The Revenge' trailer is out: Ranveer Singh's Hamza is an absolute menace - watch
content: The highly anticipated trailer for 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has officially dropped, amplifying fan excitement. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film stars Ranveer Singh as Hamza Ali Mazari, a rising gang leader in Pakistan, and features Arjun Rampal as the antagonist. The espionage thriller, set for a March 19, 2026 release, promises to delve into Hamza's backstory as an Indian spy. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'Sholay' rerun: Girl climbs mobile tower in Bihar, demands lover’s release from police custody
content: In a gripping scenario in Bihar, a young woman, just 20 years old, scaled a telecommunications tower, insistent on the release of her boyfriend, who had been taken into custody after her family lodged an abduction complaint. The tense standoff stretched on for hours as the police negotiated, finally bringing the boyfriend to the scene.

title: No confidence motion against Om Birla: TMC MPs likely to vote in favour of resolution
content: Trinamool Congress MPs will now back the no-confidence motion against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, a shift from their earlier stance, following party chief Mamata Banerjee's directive. This move intensifies the political battle as the Lok Sabha prepares to vote on March 9. Opposition parties accuse Birla of partisan conduct, jeopardizing the House's proper functioning and the rights of members.

title: 'Jasprit Bumrah is human as well': Phillips fires fresh warning before final
content: New Zealand, a nation of just 5.3 million, has achieved remarkable cricket success, reaching eight ICC finals in the past decade and winning the 2021 World Test Championship. Ahead of the T20 World Cup final against India, Glenn Phillips highlighted the contrast in talent pools but praised India’s depth. He warned New Zealand won’t underestimate Varun Chakravarthy or Jasprit Bumrah.

title: Journalist murder case: Court acquits Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim
content: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has acquitted Gurmeet Ram Rahim in the journalist Ram Chander Chhatrapati murder case, overturning a 2019 life sentence. While the Dera Sacha Sauda chief was acquitted, three other accused had their convictions upheld. The court's decision came after an appeal challenging the CBI's investigation and evidence.

title: 'This is humiliation': Congress slams PM Modi's silence over US 'permission' on Russian oil
content: Congress party has strongly criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The party stated the US gave India 'permission' to buy Russian oil. This move is seen as a humiliation for India. Congress demands an explanation from the Prime Minister. The US Treasury Secretary confirmed the permission to ease global oil supplies.

title: Bhopal teacher dies after consuming bhang at Holi gathering with students
content: A 26-year-old teaching faculty member died under suspicious circumstances after allegedly consuming bhang during a Holi gathering with students. Krishna Nayak reportedly consumed multiple glasses of bhang, leading to him feeling unwell and later dying at a hospital. The exact cause of death is pending a post-mortem report.

title: Sold as a child bride, escaped at 15: How Payal Jangid became voice against child marriage
content: In Rajasthan, teenager Payal Jangid defied tradition by refusing her child marriage at 15. Her courageous act sparked a movement, transforming her into a prominent young voice advocating against child marriage. She now champions girls' rights and education on global stages, inspiring others to challenge deeply rooted social norms.

title: 'That phase was very challenging': Samson makes honest admission
content: Sanju Samson has been one of India’s standout players in the World Cup, scoring 97* against West Indies and 89 against England in the semifinal. The 31-year-old credits his experience and clarity about his role for the performances. After years of inconsistent opportunities, Samson has finally made a strong impact and is now shortlisted for Player of the Tournament.

title: 'Indians been good actors': Why US agreed to let India buy Russian oil again
content: The US has granted India a temporary waiver to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea, aiming to stabilise global supplies amid West Asian conflict disruptions. This move, praised by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, allows India to ease short-term supply pressures while maintaining its commitment to reducing sanctioned Russian oil imports.

title: 'It felt like small tremor': 21 injured as mosque balcony wall collapses in Jaipur
content: A balcony wall at Firdaus Masjid in Jaipur collapsed shortly after Friday prayers, injuring at least 21 people. The incident occurred around 2:30 pm as devotees were exiting the mosque. Preliminary investigations suggest structural issues, possibly exacerbated by worshippers leaning on the aging, single-brick structure.

title: T20 WC: From trump card to major concern? Varun Chakravarthy losing grip at wrong time
content: With 13 wickets to his name, Varun Chakravarthy is currently the joint-leading wicket-taker of the 2026 T20 World Cup. In the first four matches, he picked up nine wickets at an economy rate of 5.17. In the last four games, however, he has leaked runs at 11.63 while picking up just four wickets. The mystery spinner is struggling with his lengths and has been taken to the cleaners, which is not a good sign for India ahead of the final against New Zealand.

title: Plumes of smoke, planes ablaze: Iran's Mehrabad airport on fire as Israel launches airstrike
content: Israeli forces launched broad-scale strikes on targets in Tehran, including Mehrabad International Airport, with explosions reported across the city and surrounding areas. This escalation follows Iran's retaliatory missile and drone attacks against Israel and Gulf countries, intensifying the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

title: Delhi's Uttam Nagar on edge after Holi killing: CRPF deployed amid heighted communal tensions
content: Two days after a fatal Holi dispute in Delhi's Uttam Nagar, a car and bike, allegedly belonging to an accused's relative, were torched, escalating tensions. Heavy security is now in place as police investigate the vehicle burning and have arrested eight individuals in connection with the killing.

title: US war with Iran: Trump announces quadruple production of 'exquisite weaponry'
content: Six major US defense contractors agreed to quadruple production of advanced weapons following a meeting with President Trump. This move aims to rapidly increase US stockpiles amid escalating Middle East tensions. Expansion efforts were already underway, with companies committed to strengthening American military capabilities.

title: Inside Bumrah’s constant evolution: An insatiable appetite for success
content: Jasprit Bumrah’s relentless training, including repeated yorker drills in practice, reflects his hunger to stay the best despite years of success and injuries. The 32-year-old pacer delivered another crucial performance in India’s World Cup semifinal against England, controlling the game with precise bowling. Praised by England captain Harry Brook, Bumrah continues proving why he remains India’s most reliable bowler.

title: Karnataka woman, lover held for smothering husband, staging his heart attack
content: A Bengaluru man, initially reported to have died of a heart attack, was actually smothered to death. Police exhumed his body, revealing the truth. His wife and her alleged lover have been arrested for the murder, orchestrated after the victim discovered their affair. The wife then falsely claimed a heart attack to conceal the crime.

title: 'Me bas fielding pe cricket nahi khela': Amir launches brutal attack on Kaif
content: Mohammad Amir and Mohammad Kaif are engaged in a verbal spat during the T20 World Cup 2026. Kaif accused Amir of seeking attention by criticizing India, while Amir retorted by questioning Kaif's career stats and suggesting he owes his success to Sourav Ganguly. Amir clarified his comments were opinions, not personal attacks.

title: A Brewer goes 30/30? A Giant wins the batting title? Bold predictions for every NL team
content: a year ago we predicted Milwaukee Brewers OFs Jackson Chourio and Christian Yelich would each go 30/30 . they did not earn an All-Star bid or get traded . we also predicted Philadelphia Phillies DH Kyle Schwarber would knock in 125 runs .

title: UFC 326 expert picks: Max Holloway is a tough matchup for Charles Oliveira
content: a panel of four offer picks and insight for the main event at UFC 326 . the titleholder is going for his second defense of the ceremonial title . he beat Dustin Poirier by unanimous decision in his last fight in July .

title: Aus GP: Verstappen crashes out of qualifying
content: MELBOURNE, Australia -- Four-time world champion Max Verstappen started Formula 1's new era by crashing out of the first qualifying session of the year. Verstappen spun as he hit the brakes for Turn 1 midway through the Q1 session, spinning out and bouncing across the gravel and into the wall. The Red Bull driver was unhurt, but the crash means he will start at the rear end of the grid for Sunday's Australian Grand Prix. After being asked if he was OK on the radio, Verstappen said: "The car just f------ locked the rear axles. Fantastic." Verstappen has already been a vocal critic of F1's new cars, which have created a complicated new driving style due to the emphasis on battery management with the sport's new hybrid engines. "I'm definitely not having fun at all with these cars," he said after qualifying. "I don't know. I mean, you can make up your mind, but I think if you look at the onboard, you see enough, right?"

title: An Athletic hits 50 homers? Four Royals hit 30? Bold predictions for every AL team
content: a year ago we predicted that athletics DH Brent Rooker would hit 50 home runs . he did not, but did not struggle . some predictions will look a bit silly in six months, if not sooner . there is a basis for making each of these bold stats .

title: How the top running back signings will fit in 2026
content: the top 2026 recruits at each position could fit at their new schools . he is capable of carrying a big load for the Longhorns . as a senior, he averaged close to 10 yards per carry .

title: Pre-spring QB rankings for all 68 Power 4 teams
content: college football rosters are as stable as they'll ever be as spring football gets started . 68 power-conference quarterbacks ranked based on stats, trends and recent performances . we have at least a reasonable idea of who will start for most of the teams .

title: As a 'mysterious' illness sidelines Kristaps Porzingis, the Warriors reckon with the future
content: KRISTAPS PORZINGIS ' DEBUT' with the Golden State Warriors on febr. 19 went about as one would expect from a player who'd been sidelined more than a month . he poured in two post-up jumpers over a mismatch, drilled a 30-footer and volleyball spiked a Nikola Vucevic floater . the Warriors won the 9 minutes, 48 seconds that Porzingis played by 15 points .

title: World Baseball Classic in Miami features custom hot dogs for each country in Pool D
content: javier Sanoja hits a solo home run to break the tie against Netherlands . there are five hot dogs in total, one for each team competing in pool D: Dominican Republic . fans can buy them individually or order a flight of hot dogs with one of each kind .

title: Women's Power Rankings: How Champ Week can help or hurt the top teams
content: the 2025-26 ESPN women's college basketball Power Rankings celebrates their fifth-straight SEC regular season title . the 2026 regular-season champions are also the defending champs of their respective 2025 tournaments .

title: NHL trade tracker: All the deals made before Friday's deadline
content: the deadline for the 2025-26 NHL season has passed . you'll find information on every trade made since the start of the regular season . the most recent trades are listed here, with the most recently on the list .

title: Ranked: The 10 worst Premier League teams that spent too much money on being bad
content: the "ESPN FC" team discuss how they will balance their relegation battle . the club finished last season in 17th place in their own domestic league . it seemed to prove that the "Big Six" clubs in England really are too big to fail .

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Iowa still looks tourney-bound; streaking USF needs some help
content: we'll use a variety of metrics as guides, classifying teams based on how likely they are to make the field of 68 as one of the 37 at-large selections . the selection committee will evaluate a team's underlying potential .
